rules.rule: Review GitHub Actions as release-critical infrastructure. Detect invalid YAML or expressions, missing permissions, unsafe pull_request_target usage, execution of untrusted PR code, secret exposure, mutable third-party action references, duplicate release workflows, incorrect APK signing, artifact/release inconsistencies, missing failure propagation, broken concurrency, unsupported runner assumptions, and events that can trigger loops or spend quota without authorization. Verify that PR workflows do not publish releases or overwrite maintainer-provided release notes.

Treat test compilation and determinism as mandatory. Verify every constructor and function call supplies all required arguments, Kotlin overload resolution is valid, coroutine tests use the correct scheduler and dispatcher, temporary resources are cleaned up, assertions test observable behavior, timeouts cannot hang CI, and tests do not depend on network, wall-clock timing, locale, execution order, or shared mutable global state unless explicitly isolated. Flag tests that compile but cannot detect the regression they claim to cover.

Treat test compilation and determinism as mandatory. Verify every constructor and function call supplies all required arguments, Kotlin overload resolution is valid, coroutine tests use the correct scheduler and dispatcher, temporary resources are cleaned up, assertions test observable behavior, timeouts cannot hang CI, and tests do not depend on network, wall-clock timing, locale, execution order, or shared mutable global state unless explicitly isolated. Flag tests that compile but cannot detect the regression they claim to cover.

Review Gradle Kotlin DSL for configuration-time failures, incompatible AGP/Kotlin/Gradle/JDK versions, duplicated plugins or dependencies, dynamic or unpinned versions, incorrect build types, broken signing configuration, leaked credentials, variant-specific source set mistakes, release-only failures, task ordering bugs, dependency conflicts, packaging regressions, and configuration that makes PR APKs use a different application ID or signing identity from normal release APKs.

Review Gradle configuration for syntax and configuration-time failures, incompatible toolchain versions, duplicated plugins or dependencies, dynamic versions, incorrect build types, broken signing, leaked credentials, variant mistakes, release-only failures, task ordering bugs, dependency conflicts, packaging regressions, and inconsistent PR versus release APK identity.

Review Android manifest changes for invalid components or attributes, exported component exposure, missing permissions, foreground-service type mismatches, deep-link hijacking, backup/data extraction exposure, cleartext traffic, provider authority collisions, process and lifecycle mistakes, package visibility failures, Android version compatibility, and services or receivers that cannot be instantiated.

Review Android resources for invalid or ignored qualifier directories, duplicate resource names, missing default resources, malformed XML, broken format arguments, incorrect escaping, untranslated or misleading user-visible errors, unsupported attributes, accessibility regressions, theme incompatibilities, and references to resources that do not exist in every required configuration. Explicitly catch locale directory typos such as alues-uk instead of values-uk.

Perform a production-grade Android/Kotlin review. Prioritize compile errors, undefined references, missing required arguments, wrong nullability, invalid smart casts, incorrect overloads, unreachable branches, state corruption, races, deadlocks, leaks, and user-visible regressions. For coroutines, preserve CancellationException propagation, mark failed runtimes unusable before rethrowing timeout cancellation, avoid swallowing cancellation, check structured concurrency, dispatcher correctness, Mutex and atomicity, stale jobs, duplicate collectors, retry storms, and concurrent cache mutation. For Jetpack Compose, detect unstable parameters, per-tick whole-screen recomposition, incorrect remember keys, stale captured lambdas, state writes during composition, expensive drawing or allocation, gesture-only controls without semantics or setProgress, missing content descriptions, lifecycle-unaware collection, and Canvas work that overheats mobile devices. For Media3 and playback services, verify player thread confinement, callback cleanup, media session consistency, queue/index restoration, seek bounds, duration unknown handling, audio focus, foreground service lifecycle, download resumption, cache keys, prefetch cancellation, and fallback behavior. For Room and persistence, verify migrations, transactions, DAO query correctness, conflict behavior, consistency across multi-step writes, and I/O off the main thread. For networking and extraction, verify response bodies close, timeouts and retries are bounded, URLs and headers are valid, cache invalidation is safe, failures do not poison reusable runtimes, secrets and tokens are never logged, and fallbacks do not silently degrade playback. Report only concrete, actionable findings with exact failure scenarios and corrected behavior.

Review Java for compile correctness, null safety, resource closure, thread safety, lifecycle leaks, blocking work on UI threads, incorrect exception handling, cancellation loss, unsafe reflection, API-level compatibility, and integration regressions with Kotlin callers.

Review shrinker rules for release-only crashes, overbroad keep rules, missing serialization or reflection members, Media3 and Room compatibility, stripped native bindings, leaked internal implementation, and rules that disable optimization unnecessarily.

Review documentation for contradictions with the actual build and release flow, obsolete download links, claims unsupported by the repository, incorrect commands or paths, security-sensitive instructions, version mismatches, and guidance that would make users install an unsigned, incompatible, or non-updatable APK.
